title: Java Annotations
content:
### Annotations in Java

Annotations are metadata tags that provide information about code. They don't directly affect execution.

---

### Built-in Annotations

| Annotation | Purpose |
|------------|---------|
| `@Override` | Check method overrides parent |
| `@Deprecated` | Mark element as deprecated |
| `@SuppressWarnings` | Suppress compiler warnings |
| `@FunctionalInterface` | Mark functional interface |
| `@SafeVarargs` | Suppress varargs warnings |

---

### Creating Custom Annotations

```java
import java.lang.annotation.*;

@Retention(RetentionPolicy.RUNTIME)
@Target(ElementType.METHOD)
public @interface MyAnnotation {
    String value() default "";
    int priority() default 1;
}
```

---

### Meta-Annotations

| Annotation | Description |
|------------|-------------|
| `@Retention` | When annotation is available |
| `@Target` | Where annotation can be applied |
| `@Documented` | Include in javadoc |
| `@Inherited` | Subclass inherits annotation |
| `@Repeatable` | Can be applied multiple times |

---

### Retention Policy

| Policy | Available At |
|--------|-------------|
| `SOURCE` | Compile time only (discarded) |
| `CLASS` | In class file, not at runtime |
| `RUNTIME` | Accessible via reflection at runtime |

---

### Target Types

```java
@Target({ElementType.METHOD, ElementType.FIELD, ElementType.TYPE})
```

| Type | Target |
|------|--------|
| `TYPE` | Class, interface, enum |
| `FIELD` | Field |
| `METHOD` | Method |
| `PARAMETER` | Parameter |
| `CONSTRUCTOR` | Constructor |

> **Key Point:** Annotations at `RUNTIME` retention can be read via reflection. Annotation elements are declared as methods in the annotation interface with optional default values.

quiz.question: Which retention policy allows reading annotations at runtime?
What does @Override do?
options: Checks correct overriding at compile time
